work_package_result.summary: Pinned Buf generation for committed Rust and TypeScript clients, added deterministic stale-artifact and strict-lint-debt checks, and enforced checker-owned WIRE_JSON compatibility against the exact PR or push base commit.

passed: just generate followed by SHA-256 comparison: all 7 generated artifacts unchanged
clean detached checkout at c4ea4ac: just bootstrap and just test-contracts with no tracked diff
Buf lint plus exact fingerprint of 10 grandfathered findings
negative lint probe adds a violation inside an ignored legacy file and is rejected
negative breaking probe reuses a field number with an incompatible type and is rejected
17 tooling unit tests and 2 Python adapter-host tests
Rust format, clippy and workspace tests
desktop, mobile and Node adapter-host TypeScript checks
not_run: generated clients are not compiled by a production consumer in this package

review.method: R2 detached review in a fresh Codex context with a focused closure pass by the original reviewer
findings_closed: PR checks now compare with github.event.pull_request.base.sha and push checks with github.event.before
Candidate and baseline schemas are reconstructed with a checker-owned WIRE_JSON config; protocols/buf.yaml has an approval hash
Module-wide lint exceptions became file-scoped and exact legacy findings are protected by a negative strict-lint probe
Every third-party action in protocol-compatibility.yml is pinned to a reviewed full commit SHA

observability_changes: Stale generation failures list each missing, unexpected or changed generated artifact path
Compatibility success reports the resolved base ref and commit SHA
Legacy lint debt changes list the exact path, Buf rule and message
known_limitations: The pre-M00 dennett.v1 package layout remains until DEBT-0001 is repaid before production consumers
Legacy service and RPC names remain until DEBT-0002 is repaid in the same protocol-epoch package
Generated Rust and TypeScript runtime dependencies are intentionally deferred to the first consumer package
